summary: PR #2366 decompiled fighter code in Kirby copy ability cleanup/hat setup and common fighter DeadUp/Rebirth behavior. The PR body claimed 5 matched functions, while the loaded bot report substantiates 3 new 100% matches and 2 partial improvements.

key_files.role: Replaced placeholders with implementations for Kirby copy-ability Yoshi hat setup and two copy-ability cleanup dispatch functions.
evidence_refs: text_corpus.jsonl issue_comment: ftKb_SpecialN_800F19AC and ftKb_SpecialN_800F1A8C reported 100%; ftKb_SpecialN_800F10D4 reported 81.20%

key_files.role: Added common fighter DeadUpFall physics and RebirthWait transition implementation, plus needed includes.
evidence_refs: text_corpus.jsonl issue_comment: ftCo_800D5600 reported 100%; ftCo_DeadUpFall_Phys reported 99.97%

key_files.role: Recovered typed fields in ftCommon_MotionVars.unk_deadup for DeadUpFall physics state.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: unk_deadup pad split into x4C float, x50 Vec3, x5C Vec3

key_files.role: Added prototype for fn_800D54A4 used as a Rebirth platform callback.

what_changed: Implemented ftKb_SpecialN_800F19AC and ftKb_SpecialN_800F1A8C as switch dispatchers on fp->fv.kb.hat.kind, calling copy-ability cleanup handlers for Popo, Peach, Fox/Falco, Link/Clink, Mewtwo, Ness, Samus, Seak, Yoshi, Donkey, and GameWatch.
Implemented ftKb_SpecialN_800F10D4 to initialize Kirby's Yoshi copy-ability hat resources, allocate hat data, build part/animation state, and call ftCo_8009D81C.
Implemented ftCo_DeadUpFall_Phys using typed motion variables for interpolation and accumulated velocity during DeadUpFall cases 1 and 3.
Implemented ftCo_800D5600 to transition a fighter to RebirthWait, reset collision/velocity, set state flags, and choose accessory callback fn_800D54A4 or fn_800D55B4.
Updated ftCommon_MotionVars.unk_deadup by replacing part of pad_x48 with x4C, x50, and x5C fields used by ftCo_DeadUpFall_Phys.
Added includes for ftanim, lbvector, and mpcoll in ft_0D31.c and exposed fn_800D54A4 in ft_0D31.h.
smart_moves: The Kirby cleanup functions use a switch over FighterKind and group shared cases such as Fox/Falco and Link/Clink instead of duplicating separate branch bodies.
DeadUpFall physics converts raw motion-variable storage into typed float and Vec3 fields before using them in source, aligning with typed-field recovery.
The RebirthWait transition updates the header for fn_800D54A4 instead of relying only on a source-local undeclared callback reference.

decomp_lessons.lesson: When a function dispatches behavior by fighter kind, a grouped switch can be a readable matching shape, especially when several adjacent kinds share the same handler sequence.
Recovering motion-variable fields from padding can make physics code express vector operations directly with Vec3 and helper calls.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: types.h unk_deadup x4C/x50/x5C field additions
raw_diff: ftCo_DeadUpFall_Phys uses lbVector_Lerp and lbVector_Add with unk_deadup fields

decomp_lessons.lesson: Prototype surfaces should be updated when a newly implemented body needs to reference an existing callback from another function.

naming_conventions.pattern: Address-style names retained for uncertain functions and fields, e.g. fn_800D54A4, ftCo_800D5600, unk_deadup.x4C/x50/x5C.
naming_conventions.notes: No PR evidence explains semantic meanings for these names, so the record should not infer stronger names.

naming_conventions.pattern: Clear local role names are used where roles are obvious, e.g. gobj, fp, kind.
naming_conventions.notes: This is consistent with conservative naming, but no reviewer feedback states it as a PR-specific convention.

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Scoped pragma dont_inline and PAD_STACK used around ftKb_SpecialN_800F10D4.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: #pragma push / #pragma dont_inline on / PAD_STACK(8) / #pragma pop in ftKb_SpecialN_800F10D4
text_corpus.jsonl issue_comment: ftKb_SpecialN_800F10D4 improved to 81.20%, not 100%
assembly_or_matching_tactics.notes: Loaded evidence does not prove the pragma/PAD_STACK are necessary or fully matching; standards would require narrow verifier evidence before treating this as durable style.

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Grouped switch dispatch for repeated fighter-kind cleanup call patterns.
assembly_or_matching_tactics.notes: Supported by bot report for these two functions, but no assembly diff excerpt is loaded.

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Typed Vec3 fields and vector helpers used instead of leaving all DeadUpFall motion state as padding/raw storage.
assembly_or_matching_tactics.notes: The bot report shows this function was still 99.97%, so the exact remaining mismatch is not available in the slice.

review_feedback.feedback: Report for GALE01 showed matched code 61.12% (+0.02%, +684 bytes), with 3 new 100% matches and 2 improved but still unmatched items.
No human review comments or reviews are present in the loaded evidence.

accepted_candidate_records.title: Grouped FighterKind switch for Kirby copy-ability cleanup dispatch
accepted_candidate_records.text: PR #2366 used grouped switch statements over fp->fv.kb.hat.kind to match ftKb_SpecialN_800F19AC and ftKb_SpecialN_800F1A8C, with shared handlers for paired fighter kinds such as Fox/Falco and Link/Clink.

accepted_candidate_records.title: Recover typed motion variables before physics helper calls
accepted_candidate_records.text: PR #2366 split unk_deadup padding into a float and two Vec3 fields, then used those fields in DeadUpFall physics with lbVector_Lerp and lbVector_Add.
